answers:
**1. What base branch should this PR target, and why?**

The PR should target **`main`**, not `feat/weighted-scorer`, even though the branch was physically built on top of `feat/weighted-scorer`. The parent branch is dead as a base: `git merge-base --is-ancestor feat/weighted-scorer main` returns false (its commits `d6fdbc3`, `2d3029b`, `ac9d505` were never merged as-is), but `git diff main feat/weighted-scorer` is **empty** — its entire content already landed on main via the squash commit `1971f08 "Refactor scorer for weighted metrics (#42)"`. The branch still existing on the remote (`git ls-remote origin` shows `refs/heads/feat/weighted-scorer` at `ac9d505`) proves nothing about it being a valid target. Basing the PR on `feat/weighted-scorer` would stack it on a branch whose own PR is finished; basing on `main` after cleanup gives reviewers exactly the pairwise work.

**2. Where was `feat/pairwise-metric` created from, and what is that starting point's status relative to main?**

The commit graph (`git log --oneline --graph --all`) shows `feat/pairwise-metric` (`1bfecb6` "X2", `7b2e100` "X1") was created from the tip of **`feat/weighted-scorer`** (`ac9d505` "W3"), which itself forked from `65204ad` "base scoring pipeline" — also the merge-base of `feat/pairwise-metric` and `main` (`git merge-base` → `65204ad`). Status of that starting point: `feat/weighted-scorer` was **squash-merged into main** as `1971f08` (#42). Evidence: its tree is byte-identical to main's tip (`git diff main feat/weighted-scorer` is empty; blob hashes `1a65b3b`/`e5e5105` match on both sides), yet none of W1–W3 are ancestors of main. Meaning for how to proceed: this is the classic dead-base situation. The branch's history carries five commits main doesn't have, three of which (W1–W3) duplicate content main already contains under a different commit ID. I must not open a PR from the branch as-is against `feat/weighted-scorer`, and against `main` the PR diff would be polluted (see Q3); the branch needs to be rebased so only the pairwise commits remain.

**3. What diff would this branch introduce against main, and is it all pairwise-metric work?**

Two different diffs matter:

- The **PR-style (merge-base) diff**, `git diff main...feat/pairwise-metric`: 3 files, 21 insertions, 3 deletions — `prompts/grader.txt` (+1), `scoring/metrics.py` (+10), `scoring/scorer.py` (+13/−3). This is what a reviewer would see, and it is **not** all pairwise work: all of `scoring/scorer.py` and 6 of the 10 lines in `scoring/metrics.py` (the `overlap` helper and weighted-aggregation changes) are the W1–W3 weighted-scorer changes, present only because the branch forked from `65204ad`, before the squash landed.
- The **actual content diff**, `git diff main feat/pairwise-metric`: only 5 insertions in 2 files — a new `pairwise_preference(answer_a, answer_b, reference)` function appended to `scoring/metrics.py` (commit X1, `7b2e100`) and one line "For pairwise comparisons, reply with A or B." appended to `prompts/grader.txt` (commit X2, `1bfecb6`).

So the genuine pairwise-metric work is just X1 + X2; the rest of the apparent diff is the already-merged weighted-scorer content showing up redundantly because the squash merge (`1971f08`) rewrote it under a new commit ID that shares no history with W1–W3.

**4. What must be done to the branch, in what order, before opening the PR?**

1. **Sync with the remote** (`git fetch origin`) and re-verify tips — I confirmed via `git ls-remote origin` that local `main` (`1971f08`), `feat/pairwise-metric` (`1bfecb6`), and the remote agree today, but that must be re-checked at execution time. Working tree is already clean (`git status --short --branch` shows nothing uncommitted).
2. **Transplant only the pairwise commits onto current main**: `git rebase --onto main feat/weighted-scorer feat/pairwise-metric`. This replays exactly X1 (`7b2e100`) and X2 (`1bfecb6`) onto `1971f08`, dropping W1–W3 (whose content main already has). It will apply conflict-free: main's `scoring/metrics.py` blob (`1a65b3b`) and `prompts/grader.txt` blob (`e5e5105`) are exactly the pre-image blobs recorded in X1's and X2's diffs. (A plain `git rebase main` would instead try to replay W1–W3 too and conflict/no-op messily — `--onto` is the correct form.)
3. **Verify the rebased branch**: `git log main..feat/pairwise-metric --oneline` should list exactly two commits, and `git diff main...feat/pairwise-metric` should now equal the 5-line content diff (2 files, 5 insertions) with `scoring/scorer.py` absent — the skill's "diff scoped to what you meant to change" gate. Run the test suite (`tests/test_scoring.py`) on the rebased branch.
4. **Force-push the rebased branch** to its own remote ref: `git push --force-with-lease origin feat/pairwise-metric` (`--force-with-lease` because the rebase rewrote history and I must not clobber anything pushed since my last look).
5. **Open the PR with base `main`**, head `feat/pairwise-metric`, checking the repo's attribution policy for the PR body before submitting, and get an actual review — never self-merge.
report:
I have everything I need. The blob hashes confirm main's files are byte-identical to the pre-images of the X1/X2 patches, so the pairwise commits transplant onto main conflict-free.

## Plan

The task: prepare (but not execute) a PR for `feat/pairwise-metric` in a repo built on top of other in-progress work. I set out to (a) locate the repo containing that branch, (b) verify true local-vs-remote state before trusting anything, (c) trace where the branch was cut from and whether that starting point is still alive relative to `main`, (d) inspect both the PR-style (merge-base) diff and the actual content diff against `main`, and (e) derive the exact remediation sequence needed before opening the PR. The central hypothesis to test, given the branch "was built with a dependency on other in-progress work," was the dead-base PR trap: a parent feature branch that was separately merged into main (possibly squashed), leaving this branch stacked on a corpse.

## Actions

Guides consulted: `.claude/skills/git-change-control-for-agents/SKILL.md` (read in full first; its Step 0 state-verification checklist and "dead-base PR trap" section shaped the whole approach).

1. `ls -la` on the workspace + `cat README.md` + `git branch -a` across all directories — found `feat/pairwise-metric` lives in `answer-scoring-pipeline/`, with its remote being the local bare repo `answer-scoring-pipeline-origin.git`.
2. In `answer-scoring-pipeline/`: `git status --short --branch` (clean tree, on `feat/pairwise-metric`), `git ls-remote origin` (used instead of `git fetch` to stay read-only; local and remote tips match exactly), `git log --oneline --graph --all -30` (revealed the topology: main at `1971f08`, a W1–W3 + X1–X2 chain forked from `65204ad`), `git worktree list` (one worktree).
3. `git branch -vv`, `git merge-base main feat/pairwise-metric` (→ `65204ad`), `git merge-base --is-ancestor feat/weighted-scorer main` (→ NO), `git diff --stat main feat/weighted-scorer` (→ **empty**), `git show --stat 1971f08` (squash commit "#42" touches `scoring/metrics.py` + `scoring/scorer.py`).
4. `git log main..feat/pairwise-metric --oneline` (5 commits: W1–W3 + X1–X2), `git diff --stat main...feat/pairwise-metric` (three-dot/PR-style: 3 files, 21+/3−), `git diff --stat main feat/pairwise-metric` (two-dot/actual: 2 files, 5+).
5. `git show 7b2e100 1bfecb6` (full X1/X2 patches), `git diff main feat/pairwise-metric` (full content diff), `git show --stat` on W1–W3.
6. `git rev-parse` on blob hashes: `main:scoring/metrics.py` = `1a65b3b` and `main:prompts/grader.txt` = `e5e5105`, identical to `feat/weighted-scorer`'s blobs and identical to the pre-image hashes in X1/X2's diffs — proving X1/X2 rebase onto main conflict-free.

(`gh pr list` was not run: origin is a local bare repository, not GitHub, so there is no PR API to query.)

## Answers

**1. What base branch should this PR target, and why?**

The PR should target **`main`**, not `feat/weighted-scorer`, even though the branch was physically built on top of `feat/weighted-scorer`. The parent branch is dead as a base: `git merge-base --is-ancestor feat/weighted-scorer main` returns false (its commits `d6fdbc3`, `2d3029b`, `ac9d505` were never merged as-is), but `git diff main feat/weighted-scorer` is **empty** — its entire content already landed on main via the squash commit `1971f08 "Refactor scorer for weighted metrics (#42)"`. The branch still existing on the remote (`git ls-remote origin` shows `refs/heads/feat/weighted-scorer` at `ac9d505`) proves nothing about it being a valid target. Basing the PR on `feat/weighted-scorer` would stack it on a branch whose own PR is finished; basing on `main` after cleanup gives reviewers exactly the pairwise work.

**2. Where was `feat/pairwise-metric` created from, and what is that starting point's status relative to main?**

The commit graph (`git log --oneline --graph --all`) shows `feat/pairwise-metric` (`1bfecb6` "X2", `7b2e100` "X1") was created from the tip of **`feat/weighted-scorer`** (`ac9d505` "W3"), which itself forked from `65204ad` "base scoring pipeline" — also the merge-base of `feat/pairwise-metric` and `main` (`git merge-base` → `65204ad`). Status of that starting point: `feat/weighted-scorer` was **squash-merged into main** as `1971f08` (#42). Evidence: its tree is byte-identical to main's tip (`git diff main feat/weighted-scorer` is empty; blob hashes `1a65b3b`/`e5e5105` match on both sides), yet none of W1–W3 are ancestors of main. The branch's history carries five commits main doesn't have, three of which (W1–W3) duplicate content main already contains under a different commit ID. I must not open a PR from the branch as-is against `feat/weighted-scorer`, and against `main` the PR diff would be polluted (see Q3); the branch needs to be rebased so only the pairwise commits remain.

**3. What diff would this branch introduce against main, and is it all pairwise-metric work?**

Two different diffs matter:

- The **PR-style (merge-base) diff**, `git diff main...feat/pairwise-metric`: 3 files, 21 insertions, 3 deletions — `prompts/grader.txt` (+1), `scoring/metrics.py` (+10), `scoring/scorer.py` (+13/−3). This is what a reviewer would see, and it is **not** all pairwise work: all of `scoring/scorer.py` and 6 of the 10 lines in `scoring/metrics.py` (the `overlap` helper and weighted-aggregation changes) are the W1–W3 weighted-scorer changes, present only because the branch forked from `65204ad`, before the squash landed.
- The **actual content diff**, `git diff main feat/pairwise-metric`: only 5 insertions in 2 files — a new `pairwise_preference(answer_a, answer_b, reference)` function appended to `scoring/metrics.py` (commit X1, `7b2e100`) and one line "For pairwise comparisons, reply with A or B." appended to `prompts/grader.txt` (commit X2, `1bfecb6`).

So the genuine pairwise-metric work is just X1 + X2; the rest of the apparent diff is the already-merged weighted-scorer content showing up redundantly because the squash merge (`1971f08`) rewrote it under a new commit ID that shares no history with W1–W3.

**4. What must be done to the branch, in what order, before opening the PR?**

1. **Sync with the remote** (`git fetch origin`) and re-verify tips — I confirmed via `git ls-remote origin` that local `main` (`1971f08`), `feat/pairwise-metric` (`1bfecb6`), and the remote agree today, but that must be re-checked at execution time. Working tree is already clean (`git status --short --branch` shows nothing uncommitted).
2. **Transplant only the pairwise commits onto current main**: `git rebase --onto main feat/weighted-scorer feat/pairwise-metric`. This replays exactly X1 (`7b2e100`) and X2 (`1bfecb6`) onto `1971f08`, dropping W1–W3 (whose content main already has). It will apply conflict-free: main's `scoring/metrics.py` blob (`1a65b3b`) and `prompts/grader.txt` blob (`e5e5105`) are exactly the pre-image blobs recorded in X1's and X2's diffs. (A plain `git rebase main` would instead try to replay W1–W3 too and conflict/no-op messily — `--onto` is the correct form.)
3. **Verify the rebased branch**: `git log main..feat/pairwise-metric --oneline` should list exactly two commits, and `git diff main...feat/pairwise-metric` should now equal the 5-line content diff (2 files, 5 insertions) with `scoring/scorer.py` absent — the skill's "diff scoped to what you meant to change" gate. Run the test suite (`tests/test_scoring.py`) on the rebased branch.
4. **Force-push the rebased branch** to its own remote ref: `git push --force-with-lease origin feat/pairwise-metric` (`--force-with-lease` because the rebase rewrote history and I must not clobber anything pushed since my last look).
5. **Open the PR with base `main`**, head `feat/pairwise-metric`, checking the repo's attribution policy for the PR body before submitting, and get an actual review — never self-merge.

## Next steps

If allowed to make changes I would execute exactly the sequence in Answer 4: fetch and re-verify, `git rebase --onto main feat/weighted-scorer feat/pairwise-metric`, confirm the two-commit/5-line diff and run the tests, `git push --force-with-lease`, then open the PR against `main` with a body noting the branch was re-based off the squash-merged `feat/weighted-scorer` (#42). I would also suggest deleting the stale `feat/weighted-scorer` remote branch (with the owner's confirmation) so no one else stacks on it.

## Confidence and gaps

- **Q1 (base = main): high confidence.** The empty tree-diff between main and `feat/weighted-scorer` plus the squash commit's `(#42)` subject is direct evidence the parent's work already landed.
- **Q2 (created from feat/weighted-scorer tip; parent squash-merged): high confidence.** Graph topology, merge-base, ancestry check, and identical blob hashes all corroborate. Minor gap: I inferred "squash merge via PR #42" from the commit subject and identical trees; the origin is a local bare repo with no PR metadata I could query (`gh` not applicable), so I cannot literally see the merged PR record.
- **Q3 (diff contents): high confidence.** Both diffs were inspected line-by-line; the split between W and X content is exact.
- **Q4 (procedure): high confidence** in the rebase applying cleanly — the pre-image blob hashes match main's blobs exactly, which is stronger than eyeballing patch context. Gaps: I could not run `git fetch` (read-only constraint), so my remote view is a point-in-time `ls-remote` snapshot; state must be re-verified before acting. I also did not run the test suite (it would be part of the change-time verification, and running arbitrary project code wasn't needed for this analysis), and there is no CI/PR tooling in this fixture to validate the final "PR reviews cleanly" claim end-to-end.

One unrelated note: the harness reported that the claude.ai Google Drive MCP server needs authentication (via your claude.ai connector settings) before its tools can be used in a future session — it was not needed for this task.
